audit_items.proof_trace: All pointer dereferences are safe: argv[optind] accesses are guarded by the bounds check at line 90 (optind >= argc returns EOF), C standard guarantees argv[0..argc-1] are non-NULL, and the only parameter without an explicit null guard (opts at line 101) is always passed as a non-NULL string literal by every caller (verified in main.c:341 and slapcommon.c:320-364). Additionally, getopt is called only at program startup before any LDAP connections are accepted, so the 'external-reachable via LDAP' reachability classification does not apply — LDAP attackers cannot influence command-line arguments.
